criteria_checked.note: The overnight envelope ruling from the v1.0.2 r1 adjudication carries over: this cycle is named in the ratified menu, approver recorded in the plan, dedicated non-main branch, critic-conditional approval, push/CI/FF withheld to morning human gates. The gate posture itself is APPROVE-compatible; the rejection below is a plan-content defect, not a gate defect.

II.5 ruling: LAWFUL. landmarks.schema.md is canonical, not a mirror (MIRRORS set verified = plan/run/verification exactly; no root LANDMARKS_SCHEMA.md exists). II.5 demands the Article III cycle for schema amendment; this plan IS that cycle (valid plan + fresh non-authoring critic + human envelope gate naming the reword item + landmark-authorized scope.lock for the contract-class file + verifier + replica/live + local commit with morning human gates). The reword is descriptive prose only; validator rules are shape/enum and unaffected. The registered deferral wording at docs/MILESTONES.md:662 verified verbatim; the current stale bullet at schema lines 33-34 verified.

Root-cause goal is right; the specified MECHANISM is defective as written — see blocker B1. The renderer mechanics themselves check out: _section maps list elements to output lines 1:1 with no re-wrapping, so DMC-repo byte-identity (zero section-7 hunk) is achievable with the correct 4-element split.

The .codex classification change is sound: the repo tracks exactly .codex/config.toml + .codex/hooks.json; current landmark scan has 187 entries with zero .codex rows; no consumer assumes a fixed landmark count or exact section-4/5 content (agents-md fixture suite checks section presence and Unknown rendering only; m6/m7/m9 suites use hand-built static landmark fixtures; release-gate ST_LANDMARKS is a static dict); L1f asserts key ABSENCE and survives; the scope-lock check at dmc-scope-lock.py:231 REFUSES a declared non-ordinary class lacking authorization; the landmark-flag sub-gate gains FLAG coverage for future .codex diffs, which is non-degrading by the v1.0.1/v1.0.2 precedent.

Reword executes the registered deferral exactly as prescribed; the reworded bullet carries no /codex/i token, so no honesty-lexeme exposure on that line; schemas-mirror selftest unaffected (file outside the mirror set).

No-regression claims verified reachable: mirror-check compares bin/lib/dmc-v0.* copies only (neither edited module is v0.x-prefixed); the frozen 802/3/3 legacy count comes from the v0.x tool set, not module selftests (v1.0.2 precedent: +44 suite rows, 802 EXACT held); landmarks selftest 11-to-12 is count-consistent with the 11 current rows.

NO-G4-override claim verified against the gate runner: G4 matches per-pathspec via git status --porcelain --

; none of the five diff paths matches any DEFAULT_PROTECTED entry. Landmark FLAG expected on bin/lib (enforcement class) + schema (contract) + MILESTONES (release) paths; scope.lock must carry landmark_authorized true for them.

blockers.statement: Proposed Change 1 as written makes the SHIPPED generator emit the companion-docs paragraph UNCONDITIONALLY on every repo it runs against. bin/lib/dmc-agents-md.py ships to hosts (dmc-install.sh:295 ships every bin/lib file), while NONE of the three referenced docs ships (SUPPORT_DOCS = the three HOST_REPO/OMC docs only; ROOT_DOCS has no AUTONOMY.md, no docs/CONTEXT_MAP.md, no docs/DMC_CONSTITUTION.md). Every host-generated AGENTS.md would therefore point at three files that do not exist in that host repo — contradicting the generated document's own charter line (repository-derived facts, never a guess), the INSTALL_MANIFEST dangling-reference discipline, and the Constitution Preamble's recorded decision that a host-shipping constitution breadcrumb is a FUTURE-AMENDMENT candidate deliberately not shipped. No machine check catches it: the m8 dangling scan covers adapters/skills/agents/DMC.md/CLAUDE.md only (bin/lib excluded), linkcheck scans skills+agents+3 delegation pointer docs only, and test-agents-md.sh never inspects section-7 body — a zero-tripwire behavior change on a shipped tool. REQUIRED CHANGE: gate the paragraph emission on the target repo actually containing the referenced companion docs (facts-driven presence check at the scanned root, emitting the identical 4 lines when present), or an equivalent repo-scoped mechanism; keep the zero-section-7-hunk byte-identity AC for the DMC repo; ADD a negative proof that a non-DMC fixture repo's generated doc omits the paragraph (which requires either amending the test-agents-md.sh no-edit row in the scope table or a module-selftest row).
blockers.evidence_ref: .claude/install/dmc-install.sh:51,295; INSTALL_MANIFEST.md:295-308; tests/fixtures/m8/test-manifest-drift.sh:101-103; bin/lib/dmc-orchestration-linkcheck.py:59-63,219-227; docs/DMC_CONSTITUTION.md Preamble; bin/lib/dmc-agents-md.py render header
